name: Quintessence (dynamical dark energy)
summary: Replaces a true cosmological constant with a slowly-evolving scalar field whose potential drives present-day cosmic acceleration. The equation-of-state w(z) deviates from -1, allowing tests against w_0-w_a CDM. DESI Y1 BAO + Type-Ia SN data show a ~2-3 sigma preference for w_0 > -1, w_a < 0, hinting at evolving dark energy.

addresses_puzzles.note: Does not solve the underlying naturalness puzzle: a quintessence potential V(phi) ~ (meV)^4 still requires explanation. Trades a single fine-tuning for a family (potential shape, initial conditions, mass), but predicts testable late-time dynamics.

touches_benchmarks.note: Evolving w(z) leaves an imprint on growth of structure separable from a cosmological constant; DESI + DESI-2 + LSST measurements are sensitive to the predicted signal.

new_symmetries: approximate shift symmetry to protect mass
introduces.note: Potential shape (e.g., V_0, alpha for inverse-power-law), initial field value, and field mass.

history: Ratra and Peebles (1988); Caldwell, Dave, Steinhardt (1998). DESI Y1 (2024) provided the first hint of dynamical dark energy in BAO+SN combinations; Pantheon+, Union3, and DES-SN5YR sample choice significantly affects the strength of the preference.

references.citation: Ratra and Peebles, 'Cosmological consequences of a rolling homogeneous scalar field', Phys. Rev. D 37, 3406 (1988)
Caldwell, Dave, Steinhardt, 'Cosmological imprint of an energy component with general equation of state', PRL 80, 1582 (1998)
DESI Collaboration, 'DESI 2024 VI: Cosmological Constraints from Baryon Acoustic Oscillations', arXiv:2404.03002
